content:
Let the line A be the x‐axis, and let circle B have center \((0,R)\) with \(R=2008\).  Any circle \(C_n\) of radius \(r_n\) tangent to the x‐axis has curvature \(k_n=1/r_n\).  The big circle B has curvature \(k_B=1/R\), and the line has curvature \(0\).  By Descartes’ theorem (choosing the “outer” solution so that \(r_2>r_1\)), one finds the recurrence

  \(k_n \;=\;k_{n-1}+k_B\;-\,2\sqrt{k_{n-1}k_B}.\)

Set \(s_n=\sqrt{k_n}\).  Then
\[
s_n^2 \;=\;\bigl(\,s_{n-1}-\sqrt{k_B}\bigr)^2
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
s_n=s_{n-1}-\sqrt{k_B}\,,
\]
and since \(s_1=\sqrt{k_1}=1\), 
\[
s_n=1-(n-1)\sqrt{\tfrac1R}
      =1-\frac{\,n-1\,}{\sqrt R}\,.
\]
We need \(s_n>0\), i.e.\ 
\[
1-\frac{n-1}{\sqrt{2008}}>0
\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
n-1<\sqrt{2008}\approx44.82
\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
n\le45.
\]
Thus the largest \(n\) for which the circles still have positive radius is \(n=45\).  

Answer: \(\boxed{45}\).

message.content:
Let the line A be the x‐axis, and let circle B have center \((0,R)\) with \(R=2008\).  Any circle \(C_n\) of radius \(r_n\) tangent to the x‐axis has curvature \(k_n=1/r_n\).  The big circle B has curvature \(k_B=1/R\), and the line has curvature \(0\).  By Descartes’ theorem (choosing the “outer” solution so that \(r_2>r_1\)), one finds the recurrence

  \(k_n \;=\;k_{n-1}+k_B\;-\,2\sqrt{k_{n-1}k_B}.\)

Set \(s_n=\sqrt{k_n}\).  Then
\[
s_n^2 \;=\;\bigl(\,s_{n-1}-\sqrt{k_B}\bigr)^2
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
s_n=s_{n-1}-\sqrt{k_B}\,,
\]
and since \(s_1=\sqrt{k_1}=1\), 
\[
s_n=1-(n-1)\sqrt{\tfrac1R}
      =1-\frac{\,n-1\,}{\sqrt R}\,.
\]
We need \(s_n>0\), i.e.\ 
\[
1-\frac{n-1}{\sqrt{2008}}>0
\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
n-1<\sqrt{2008}\approx44.82
\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
n\le45.
\]
Thus the largest \(n\) for which the circles still have positive radius is \(n=45\).  

Answer: \(\boxed{45}\).
